text: Agile methodology is a popular way to manage projects, especially in software development. Instead of planning everything at once, teams work in short phases called sprints, usually lasting two to four weeks. Each day starts with a quick standup meeting where members share what they did yesterday, what they will do today, and any problems they face. The team keeps a backlog, which is a list of tasks to complete. These tasks are often written as user stories, describing a feature from the user's perspective. This approach is called iterative development because teams repeat the cycle, improving the product step by step based on feedback.
